articles.title: ‘We don’t have another country to run to’: Kenyans fear US plan for Ebola quarantine site
articles.description:
People from town of potential site for US citizens exposed to Ebola say it puts them at risk in country with no known cases
People from a town in central Kenya where the US wants to set up an Ebola quarantine facility for its citizens have strongly criticised the plan, saying they fear it will expose them to the virus and that it is indicative of double standards on the part of the US.
“Everybody should be quarantined in their home country. We shouldn’t allow foreigners to bring us diseases,” said Charles Mathenge, a taxi driver who lives near Laikipia Air Base, the proposed site in Nanyuki, 120 miles from the capital, Nairobi. “Kenya is our country, and we should be careful with it.”
 Continue reading...
articles.titleJa: 「逃げ場はない」：ケニア人、米国によるエボラ隔離施設計画を懸念
articles.summary: 米国がケニアにエボラ隔離施設を計画。ケニア国民は感染リスクを恐れ、自国での隔離を要求し、米国への不信感を募らせている。

articles.title: Mozambique says five citizens killed in ‘xenophobic attacks’ in South Africa
articles.description:
South African police confirm two deaths of Mozambicans in Mossel Bay as anti-immigration violence sweeps country
Mozambique said five of its nationals were killed in “xenophobic attacks” in South Africa at the weekend and efforts were under way on Tuesday to repatriate hundreds of others.
However, the South African police confirmed only that two Mozambicans had died in violence in the southern coastal town of Mossel Bay, the first killings to be officially linked to a wave of anti-migrant protests sweeping the country.
 Continue reading...
articles.titleJa: モザンビーク、南アフリカでの「外国人排斥攻撃」で5人の国民が死亡と発表

articles.title: People ‘panicking’ as Ghana passes sweeping law criminalising LGBTQ+ activity
articles.description:
Community groups say some fear they could lose homes, jobs and access to healthcare if the new law is ratified by President John Dramani Mahama
Ghana’s LGBTQ community is living in fear after the country’s parliament approved a sweeping bill that criminalises the promotion of LGBTQ+ activities and identifying as lesbian, gay, bisexual, transgender or queer, rights groups have warned.
The legislation, which was passed on Friday, mandates prison sentences of three to 10 years.
 Continue reading...

articles.title: UK will not have to pay Rwanda £100m over failed asylum scheme, court rules
articles.description:
Rwanda had sued UK government over alleged breach of agreement, after scheme scrapped by Labour on first day in office
The UK will not have to pay the Rwandan government millions of pounds over a failed migrant deportation scheme set up by Boris Johnson’s administration, an international court has ruled.
The east African nation had sued the current UK government for more than £100m, claiming it was owed after a breach of an agreement.
 Continue reading...
articles.titleJa: 英国、失敗した亡命者送還計画でルワンダに1億ポンドの支払い不要と裁判所が判断

articles.title: Wildfires devastating richer areas but fewer hectares burned globally – study
articles.description:
‘Megafires’ in California, Canada, South Korea and Europe in 2025, but changes to farming slowed spread in parts of Africa
“Devastating” wildfires ripped across the wealthier parts of the world in 2025, a study has found, even as globally, the area ravaged by flames fell.
Catastrophic blazes claimed lives, homes and jobs last year in California, Canada, Europe and South Korea. But the 335m hectares burned was the second-lowest since 2002, the review found, largely owing to the expansion of African farms that have fragmented landscapes and hampered the spread of large savannah fires.
 Continue reading...
articles.titleJa: 富裕地域を荒廃させる山火事、しかし地球全体では焼失面積が減少 – 研究
articles.summary: 2025年、欧米などの富裕地域で壊滅的な山火事が発生。しかし、アフリカの農地拡大により、地球全体の焼失面積は過去20年で2番目に低かったと研究が示す。

articles.title: Kidnappings, threats and ‘protection fees’: how can Mexico confront rise in deadly extortion?
articles.description:
With corrupt police on the streets and shopkeepers forced to pay gangs, president has vowed to tackle crime that now affects all parts of society
It was about 11pm and Luis* was about to get into an Uber to go home when the police car pulled up. One of the officers produced two plastic bags with what looked like drugs: one contained some sort of powder, the other little crystals. Luis had never seen them before.
Luis, who asked not to use his real name for fear of reprisals, insisted that the drugs weren’t his, but the officers didn’t seem to care. They shoved him into the back of the police truck and drove into the night.
 Continue reading...
articles.titleJa: 誘拐、脅迫、そして「用心棒代」：メキシコはいかにして致命的な恐喝の増加に立ち向かうか？

articles.title: Trump administration proposes 25% tariffs on Brazil despite US trade surplus
articles.description:
US claims world’s 10th-biggest economy engages in ‘unreasonable’ trade practices that ‘restrict US commerce’

Sign up for the Breaking News US newsletter email

The Trump administration proposed 25% tariffs on imports from Brazil, charging that the world’s 10th-biggest economy engages in trade practices that are “unreasonable’’ and that “burden or restrict US commerce”.
Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva said he received the decision “with indignation”. The Brazil president also blamed the decision by the US administration on his rival in October’s elections, Flávio Bolsonaro, the senator who visited Washington last week. The senator is the son of former president Jair Bolsonaro, once nicknamed “the Trump of the Tropics” by his allies.
 Continue reading...

articles.title: Cricket Canada suspended over allegations of gang-linked corruption
articles.description:
ICC’s decision comes amid growing concerns the team is being influenced by members of a notorious gang in India
Cricket’s international governing body has suspended Canada over what it described as “serious breaches of its membership obligations”, dealing the latest blow to an organization that critics say has become a “laughing stock” within the sport.
The suspension also comes amid growing concerns that one of Canada’s fastest-growing sports is being influenced by members of a notorious gang that operates with impunity from an Indian prison cell.
 Continue reading...

articles.title: Musket balls and a burnt hull: evidence of real pirates of the Caribbean found in Bahamas
articles.description:
Exclusive: First shipwrecks found in Nassau harbour on New Providence, once the hideout of Blackbeard and Calico Jack
The first shipwrecks linked to the real pirates of the Caribbean in the Bahamas have been discovered by an international team co-directed by a British marine archaeologist.
Blackbeard and Calico Jack Rackham were among pirates who, between the 1690s and 1720s, turned Nassau on the island of New Providence into a hideout where they plotted their next heists on the high seas and divided up their plunder.
 Continue reading...
articles.titleJa: カリブ海のリアルな海賊の証拠、バハマで発見：マスケット弾と焦げた船体
articles.summary: バハマのナッソー港で、黒ひげなどの伝説的な海賊に関連する複数の難破船が発見された。マスケット弾や焦げた船体などが、当時の活動を裏付ける証拠となる。

articles.title: Mexico City police teargas teachers’ protest 10 days before World Cup
articles.description:
Teachers associated with CNTE union were marching toward Zócalo for salary raises and reversal of pension laws 
Riot police fired teargas at teachers who were marching toward Mexico City’s historic Zócalo plaza, just days before the square is expected to host the 2026 World Cup “Fan Fest”. The incident is the second time police have clashed with teachers in the past week, and more conflict is likely as Mexico City prepares to hold the opening game of the Fifa World Cup on 11 June.
“This event will have to be suspended,” Filiberto Frausto, a union leader, told AFP, which witnessed police firing teargas on 1 June. “A cause like ours should be far above – it’s far more important than a little bit of distraction and fun.”
 Continue reading...

articles.titleJa: 私の兄がどのようにしてリベラルなハリウッド俳優からマン・スフィアの「救世主」になったか

articles.title: Trump is putting pressure on Cuba - why and to what end?
articles.description: The US has warned that a peaceful agreement with Cuba is unlikely, Havana insists this is a "fraudulent case" designed to justify military intervention.
articles.titleJa: トランプはなぜキューバに圧力をかけているのか、そしてその目的は？
articles.summary: 米国がキューバとの平和的合意は困難と警告する中、ハバナはこれを軍事介入を正当化するための「詐欺的なケース」と主張しており、トランプ政権によるキューバへの圧力が高まっている。

articles.title: Cubans grapple with fuel shortages and blackouts as US steps up pressure
articles.description: The BBC speaks to Havana residents after the US charges the country's former leader, Raúl Castro, with murder.
articles.titleJa: 米国の圧力強化により、キューバ国民は燃料不足と停電に苦しむ
articles.summary: 米国がキューバの元指導者ラウル・カストロ氏を殺人罪で告発し、キューバへの圧力を強める中、ハバナの住民たちは深刻な燃料不足と停電に苦しんでいる。

articles.title: The seed guardian of Brazil’s Caatinga
articles.description: In Minas Gerais, Brazil, a catingueiro shares his home with the memory of his ancestors and generations of plant varieties.
articles.titleJa: ブラジルのカアチンガの種の守護者
articles.summary: ブラジルのミナスジェライス州で、「カチンゲイロ」が先祖の記憶と何世代にもわたる植物品種を守り、共有している。地域の生物多様性保全への重要な取り組み。

articles.title: Who is talking about artificial intelligence in Ecuador?
articles.description: Ecuador is debating how to regulate artificial intelligence:, institutional gaps, and tensions between innovation, surveillance, education, inclusion, and responsible public governance are at the center of the conversation.
articles.titleJa: エクアドルで人工知能について語っているのは誰か？
articles.summary: エクアドルでは人工知能の規制方法について議論が進行中。イノベーション、監視、教育、包容性、責任ある公共ガバナンスが主要な焦点となっている。

articles.title: In Barra do Turvo, Brazil, women promote biodiversity and harvest healthy food at fair prices
articles.description: A local agricultural collective in Barra do Turvo, Brazil, outside of São Paulo, is empowering women to earn an income, farm their own produce to promote self-sufficiency, and diversify crops.
articles.titleJa: ブラジル、バラ・ド・トゥルヴォで女性たちが生物多様性を促進し、公正な価格で健康的な食料を収穫
articles.summary: ブラジルのバラ・ド・トゥルヴォにある農業共同体が、女性に収入と自給自足の機会を提供し、作物の多様化を通じて生物多様性を促進している。

articles.title: Countless endangered species are making a comeback due to global conservation efforts
articles.description: These success stories are a model for community-rooted conservation and population revival that could help other countries and regions rebuild their withering ecosystems.
articles.titleJa: 世界的な保護活動により、数多くの絶滅危惧種が復活を遂げている
articles.summary: 世界的な保護活動により、数多くの絶滅危惧種が復活している。これらの成功事例は、コミュニティ主導の保全モデルとして他地域の生態系再建に役立つ。

articles.title: The death of a relentless mother reopens the wound of Venezuela’s political repression
articles.description: Carmen Navas’s passing reveals alleged institutional coverups, deepening concerns about repression, impunity, and Venezuela’s ongoing democratic collapse.
articles.titleJa: 執念深い母親の死がベネズエラの政治的抑圧の傷を再び開く
articles.summary: カルメン・ナバス氏の逝去は、ベネズエラの制度的隠蔽疑惑を明らかにし、抑圧、不処罰、そして進行中の民主主義の崩壊への懸念を深めている。

articles.title: Rosa Pachurí Paraba: How Monkox indigenous women are leading Bolivia’s response to wildfires
articles.description: Bolivia’s 2024 wildfires devastated Santa Cruz, but Indigenous Chiquitano and Monkox communities led recovery through collective organization, ancestral knowledge and fire management strategies, and revived food systems amid delayed state response.
articles.titleJa: ロサ・パチュリ・パラバ：モンコク先住民女性がボリビアの山火事対応をどのように主導しているか
articles.summary: 2024年のボリビアの山火事に対し、モンコク先住民女性が伝統知識と組織力で復興を主導。遅れる国家の対応の中、食料システムを復活させ、コミュニティの回復を牽引している。

articles.title: Attacks on trans identities become a strategic political tool in Brazil
articles.description: In the last decade in Brazil, transphobia has become one of the main tools for disseminating misinformation. In many cases, it is spread by elected politicians with millions of followers
articles.titleJa: トランスジェンダーのアイデンティティへの攻撃がブラジルで戦略的な政治的道具となる
articles.summary: ブラジルでは過去10年間、トランスフォビアが誤情報拡散の主要手段となり、多くのケースで数百万人のフォロワーを持つ選出された政治家によって広められている。

articles.title: Dominican Republic: Is protecting your environment a way to love your country?
articles.description: Three Dominican ecologists and environmental managers explore how protecting ecosystems, conserving resources, and promoting sustainability represent meaningful acts of patriotism that safeguard communities, biodiversity, and future generations across the nation.
articles.titleJa: ドミニカ共和国：環境を守ることは国を愛する方法か？
articles.summary: ドミニカ共和国のエコロジストたちが、環境保護、資源保全、持続可能性の推進が、国民の愛国心とコミュニティ、生物多様性、未来世代を守る重要な行動であることを論じる。

articles.title: Across Latin America, May Day protests highlight workers’ struggles and solidarity
articles.description: From Venezuela to Chile, May Day protests across Latin America exposed economic hardship, labor precarity,  gender inequality, and growing public frustration with governments failing to protect workers’ rights and dignity.
articles.titleJa: ラテンアメリカ各地でメーデーの抗議活動が労働者の苦闘と連帯を浮き彫りに
articles.summary: ラテンアメリカ各地のメーデー抗議活動が、経済的困難、労働不安、ジェンダー不平等、そして労働者の権利を保護できない政府への国民の不満を浮き彫りにした。

articles.title: Who is defending Cuba’s threatened species?
articles.description: Cuba hosts some of the world’s richest biodiversity but faces the impacts of climate change, human activity and weak protections. Scientists and activists strive to preserve endangered wildlife and ecosystems.
articles.titleJa: キューバの絶滅危惧種を守っているのは誰か？
articles.summary: 世界有数の生物多様性を持つキューバは、気候変動や人間活動、保護対策の不備に直面している。科学者や活動家が絶滅危惧種と生態系保全に尽力している。

articles.title: Now you can hire people to carry your shopping bags in Delhi - will it work?
articles.description: CarryMen employees don't just carry shopping bags - they also push prams and queue up at food counters.
articles.titleJa: デリーで買い物袋を運ぶ人を雇えるように - うまくいくか？
articles.summary: デリーで買い物代行サービス「キャリーメン」が登場。買い物袋を運ぶだけでなく、ベビーカーを押したり、フードカウンターに並んだりする。

articles.title: Why Myanmar president's India visit is being closely watched
articles.description: Myanmar is seeking to broaden diplomatic engagement after years of international criticism and isolation.
articles.titleJa: ミャンマー大統領のインド訪問が注視される理由
articles.summary: 長年の国際的な批判と孤立を経て、ミャンマーが外交関係を拡大しようと試みているため、インド訪問が注目されている。

articles.title: China goes after 'ghost kitchens' to rein in cut-throat food delivery apps
articles.description: The thousands of "ghost kitchens" - online shops that don't actually exist - have spooked Chinese consumers.
articles.titleJa: 中国、「ゴーストキッチン」を取り締まり、過当競争のフードデリバリーアプリを抑制
articles.summary: 中国政府が、実体のない「ゴーストキッチン」を取り締まり、競争が激化するフードデリバリーアプリ業界を規制する動きを見せている。

articles.title: Steph Curry signs with Chinese brand after Under Armour split
articles.description: Chinese firms have been vying to secure deals with big stars as they push to become global brands.
articles.titleJa: ステフィン・カリー、アンダーアーマーとの契約終了後、中国ブランドと契約
articles.summary: 中国企業がグローバルブランド化を目指し、大物スターとの契約獲得に注力する中、ステフィン・カリーが中国ブランドと契約した。

articles.title: Litterbugs now face on-the-spot fines in Tokyo's tourist hotspot
articles.description: Dozens of officials will patrol world-famous Shibuya as Japan responds to the impacts of overtourism.
articles.titleJa: 東京の観光名所でゴミのポイ捨てに即時罰金
articles.summary: オーバーツーリズムの影響に対応するため、東京の渋谷で、ゴミのポイ捨てに対する即時罰金制度が導入される。

articles.title: Nvidia announces new AI chip for personal computers
articles.description: The technology giant's boss Jensen Huang called the move the "reinvention of the computer".
articles.titleJa: Nvidia、パーソナルコンピューター向け新AIチップを発表
articles.summary: NvidiaがPC向けの新AIチップを発表。同社CEOはこれを「コンピューターの再発明」と称している。

articles.title: Huge blast kills dozens in rebel-held village in Myanmar
articles.description: Insurgents say it was caused by explosives being used for mining close to the Chinese border.
articles.titleJa: ミャンマーの反政府勢力支配下の村で大規模な爆発、数十人死亡
articles.summary: ミャンマーの反政府勢力支配下の村で大規模な爆発が発生し、数十人が死亡。中国国境近くの採掘用爆発物が原因とされている。

articles.title: Japan defence minister denies militarism and criticises China's 'huge arsenal'
articles.description: Shinjiro Koizumi makes some of Tokyo's most pointed remarks yet in the dispute over Japan's military build-up.
articles.titleJa: 日本の防衛大臣、軍国主義を否定し中国の「巨大な兵器庫」を批判
articles.summary: 日本の防衛大臣が、日本の軍事力増強に関する論争の中で、軍国主義を否定し、中国の巨大な兵器庫を批判する発言をした。

articles.title: 'Mornings and nights no longer exist' at 47C:  A day in the hottest place in India
articles.description: What a day in India's hottest district reveals about life on the frontline of extreme heat.
articles.titleJa: 47度で「朝と夜がもはや存在しない」：インドの最も暑い場所での一日
articles.summary: インドの最も暑い地域で、気温47度という過酷な状況下での生活が、極端な暑さがもたらす影響を浮き彫りにしている。

articles.title: Watch: Glass doors break as fans scramble to see Pursuit of Jade star
articles.description: Huge crowds formed outside a shopping centre in China to see actor Zhang Linghe.
articles.titleJa: 動画：『玉骨遥』スターを見ようとファンが殺到し、ガラスドアが破損
articles.summary: 中国のショッピングセンターで、ドラマ『玉骨遥』のスターを見るために集まった大勢のファンが殺到し、ガラスドアが破損する事態が発生した。

articles.title: Members share experiences and perspectives during WTO Trade and Environment Week
articles.description: The seventh edition of WTO Trade and Environment Week is taking place from 1 to 5 June at WTO headquarters in Geneva and online. The event features 16 public sessions organized by WTO members, focusing on a wide range of issues at the intersection of trade and the environment. It began with a meeting of the WTO Committee on Trade and Environment (CTE) chaired by Ambassador Ram Prasad Subedi of Nepal.
articles.titleJa: WTO貿易環境週間中にメンバーが経験と見解を共有
articles.summary: WTOジュネーブ本部で開催された第7回貿易環境週間では、貿易と環境の交差点にある様々な問題についてメンバーが意見を交わしました。

articles.title: Joint Statement by the Heads of the IEA, IMF, World Bank, and WTO
articles.description: The Heads of the International Energy Agency, International Monetary Fund, World Bank Group and World Trade Organization met on May 28 as part of the high-level coordination group established in April to maximise their institutions’ response to the energy, trade, and economic impacts of the war in the Middle East. Following the meeting, they issued the statement below:
articles.titleJa: IEA、IMF、世界銀行、WTOの代表による共同声明
articles.summary: IEA、IMF、世界銀行、WTOの各代表は、中東紛争によるエネルギー、貿易、経済への影響に対応するため、共同声明を発表しました。

articles.title: Francophone parliamentarians strengthen engagement on multilateral trade at WTO/APF event
articles.description: The WTO Secretariat and the Assemblée parlementaire de la Francophonie (APF) concluded a two-day workshop in Geneva on 28-29 May bringing together francophone parliamentarians to deepen their engagement with the WTO and the multilateral trading system.
articles.titleJa: WTO/APFイベントでフランス語圏の議員が多国間貿易への関与を強化
articles.summary: WTOとフランス語圏議会連盟はジュネーブでワークショップを開催し、多国間貿易システムへのフランス語圏議員の関与を深めました。

articles.title: Members discuss next steps for technology transfer work following MC14, elect new Chair
articles.description: The Working Group on Trade and Transfer of Technology met on 28 May to discuss how to continue advancing work on increasing flows of technology to developing economies following the WTO’s 14th Ministerial Conference (MC14) in Yaoundé in March. The newly elected Chair, Ambassador Sumathi Balakrishnan of Malaysia, told the Working Group that she will work with all members to define a constructive path forward for the Group’s work.
articles.titleJa: MC14後の技術移転作業の次のステップを議論、新議長を選出
articles.summary: 技術貿易・移転作業部会は、途上国への技術移転促進について議論し、マレーシアのスマーティ・バラクリシュナン大使を新議長に選出しました。

articles.title: New Zealand launches safeguard investigation on certain aluminium extrusions
articles.description: On 27 May 2026, New Zealand notified the WTO’s Committee on Safeguards of the initiation on 28 May 2026 of a safeguard investigation on imports of certain aluminium extrusions.
articles.titleJa: ニュージーランドが特定アルミニウム押出材に対するセーフガード調査を開始
articles.summary: ニュージーランドは、特定のアルミニウム押出材の輸入に対するセーフガード調査の開始をWTOセーフガード委員会に通知しました。

articles.title: Shipping industry cites mounting cost, capacity pressures amid global trade disruptions
articles.description: Senior executives from leading global shipping companies and industry groups underlined growing operational and economic challenges to goods trade during a 28 May meeting with WTO Director-General Ngozi Okonjo-Iweala. They noted that, while global supply chains have demonstrated resilience, efforts to identify and rely on alternative maritime routes amid disruptions – particularly in the Gulf region and other chokepoints – are driving up costs for shippers and ultimately consumers.
articles.titleJa: 世界的な貿易混乱の中、海運業界がコストと輸送能力の圧力増大を指摘
articles.summary: 海運会社幹部は、WTO事務局長との会談で、世界の貿易における運営上および経済的な課題が増大していることを強調しました。

articles.title: DDG Nordquist calls for urgent action on fisheries subsidies at Monaco Blue Initiative
articles.description: Deputy Director-General DJ Nordquist called for urgent action by WTO members to complete the acceptance and implementation of the Agreement on Fisheries Subsidies and to advance negotiations on additional disciplines targeting overcapacity and overfishing. She delivered her remarks on 27 May at the Monaco Blue Initiative in Monaco. DDG Nordquist stressed that harmful fisheries subsidies continue to undermine ocean sustainability, economic resilience, and coastal livelihoods, while emphasizing that sustainable fisheries are essential for jobs, food security, and long-term prosperity. Her full remarks are below.
articles.titleJa: ノードキスト事務次長、モナコ・ブルー・イニシアティブで漁業補助金に関する緊急行動を要請
articles.summary: ノードキストWTO事務次長はモナコで、漁業補助金協定の実施と過剰漁獲対策の交渉進展を強く促し、持続可能な漁業の重要性を強調しました。

articles.title: Members discuss agriculture domestic support, impact of El Nino and Middle East conflict
articles.description: At the first meeting of the Committee on Agriculture since the 14th Ministerial Conference in Yaoundé, Cameroon, in March, WTO members covered a wide range of issues on 27-28 May, with particular attention paid to farm support, including questions arising from members' notifications to the Committee. They also shared perspectives on determining the status of net food-importing developing countries and took note of forecasts and reports on global farming from international organizations.
articles.titleJa: 農業国内支持、エルニーニョと中東紛争の影響をメンバーが議論
articles.summary: WTO農業委員会は、農業国内支持、ネット食料輸入開発途上国の状況、エルニーニョと中東紛争が世界の農業に与える影響を議論しました。

articles.title: Trade Policy Review: Japan
articles.description: The sixteenth review of the trade policies and practices of Japan takes place on 27 and 29 May 2026. The basis for the review is a report by the WTO Secretariat and a report by the Government of Japan.
articles.titleJa: 貿易政策審査：日本
articles.summary: WTO事務局と日本政府の報告書に基づき、日本の貿易政策と慣行に関する第16回審査が実施されます。

articles.title: Members consider Chinese request for dispute panel on solar, IT goods measures in India
articles.description: At a meeting of the Dispute Settlement Body (DSB) on 22 May, members considered a request from China for the establishment of a dispute panel to review measures in India affecting imports of solar cells, solar modules and information technology goods. The new DSB Chair, Ambassador Guilherme de Aguiar Patriota (Brazil), also made a statement on advancing the WTO's dispute settlement reform talks.
articles.titleJa: 中国、インドにおける太陽光・IT製品措置に関する紛争パネル設置を要求
articles.summary: 紛争解決機関の会合で、中国はインドが太陽電池やIT製品の輸入に課している措置を審査する紛争パネルの設置を要求しました。
